informal_statement.text: Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

semantic_units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target.
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr.
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

semantic_units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat), binarySearch arr t = some i → i < arr.length ∧ arr.get? i = some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int), SortedLE arr → binarySearch arr t = none → ∀ i, i < arr.length → arr.get? i ≠ some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No additional global preconditions beyond having an array and a target; the algorithm must work for any input.
Assume the input slice/array is sorted in nondecreasing order (i.e., for all i<j, arr[i] ≤ arr[j]).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: let n := arr.size; let f := fun i : Fin n => arr[i.1]; True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Relate the array to an indexable function over Fin n (used to state correctness over valid indices only).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (res : Option (Fin arr.size)),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  (res = binarySearch arr target) →
  (match res with
   | some i => arr[i.1] = target
   | none => ∀ j : Fin arr.size, arr[j.1] ≠ target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Soundness: if the implementation returns Some i, then arr[i] equals the target; if it returns None, then the target does not occur at any valid index.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  (binarySearch arr target = none ↔ ∀ j : Fin arr.size, arr[j.1] ≠ target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Completeness for absence: under sortedness, the algorithm returns None exactly when the target is absent from the array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  (∃ j : Fin arr.size, arr[j.1] = target) →
  ∃ i : Fin arr.size, binarySearch arr target = some i ∧ arr[i.1] = target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Completeness for presence: under sortedness, if the target occurs somewhere, the algorithm returns Some at some valid matching index.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  match binarySearch arr target with
  | some i => i.1 < arr.size
  | none => True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Safety: any returned index is within bounds (Fin arr.size guarantees this, but the obligation makes it explicit).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  binarySearch terminates
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Termination: the implementation must always terminate (e.g., via a decreasing interval measure in the binary search loop/recursion).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  ∀ (lo hi : Nat),
    (* invariant holds at each step of the algorithm *)
    Invariant arr target lo hi →
    (* invariant implies the search space contains all possible matches *)
    (∃ j : Fin arr.size, arr[j.1] = target) →
    (∃ j : Fin arr.size, lo ≤ j.1 ∧ j.1 < hi ∧ arr[j.1] = target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop/recursion invariant: if the target exists anywhere, then it must lie within the current search interval (so the algorithm never discards a region that could contain the target).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  ∀ (lo hi : Nat),
    Invariant arr target lo hi →
    lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ arr.size
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Interval bounds invariant: the maintained search interval is always well-formed and within array bounds.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  ∀ (lo hi : Nat),
    Invariant arr target lo hi →
    (∀ j : Fin arr.size, j.1 < lo → arr[j.1] < target) ∧
    (∀ j : Fin arr.size, j.1 ≥ hi → arr[j.1] > target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Stronger invariant using sortedness: indices strictly left of the interval cannot contain the target (they are < target), and indices strictly right cannot contain the target (they are > target).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int),
  SortedNondecreasing arr →
  let res := binarySearch arr target
  in match res with
     | some i => arr[i.1] = target
     | none => ∀ j : Fin arr.size, arr[j.1] ≠ target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Final postcondition: combines the two cases into a single statement matching the required return behavior.
